channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: US Forces Strike Iranian Drone, Missile Sites After Attack On Cargo Ship

media:content.media:description: US said the strikes were a response to "unwarranted aggression against commercial shipping by Iran.

item.description: US forces struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar locations on Friday in response to an Iranian attack on a cargo ship, the US military said.
item.content:encoded: US forces struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar locations on Friday in response to an Iranian attack on a cargo ship, the US military said.

item.title: Hezbollah Rejects Israel-Lebanon Deal, Warns Of "Civil War"

media:content.media:description: Hezbollah said it will be unable to impose the implementation of the agreement signed in Washington.

item.description: Israel and Lebanon hailed an agreement signed Friday with the United States to pave a way towards peace on their front in the Middle East war, while Iran-backed Hezbollah warned the deal would thwart plans to resolve the broader conflict.
item.content:encoded: Israel and Lebanon hailed an agreement signed Friday with the United States to pave a way towards peace on their front in the Middle East war, while Iran-backed Hezbollah warned the deal would thwart plans to resolve the broader conflict.

item.title: "Foolish Violation": Trump Says Iran Fired 4 Drones At Ships In Hormuz, 1 Hit

media:content.media:description: Donald Trump has slammed Iran.

item.description: In a post on Truth Social, Trump said Iran had shot at least four one-way drones on ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: In a post on Truth Social, Trump said Iran had shot at least four one-way drones on ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump Blasts "Godless" Democrats In Incendiary Speech To Evangelicals

media:content.media:description: Trump said that democrats are the "most serious threat" to the US.

item.description: Speaking in Washington after the success of three progressive candidates in New York's Democratic primary elections this week, Trump called left-wing ideology an "uncontrollable form of cancer" that will ensure America is "taken down."
item.content:encoded: Speaking in Washington after the success of three progressive candidates in New York's Democratic primary elections this week, Trump called left-wing ideology an "uncontrollable form of cancer" that will ensure America is "taken down."

item.title: JD Vance Says Watergate Would Be '12-Hour News Story' Today

media:content.media:description: JD Vance says it is "crazy" that the Watergate scandal brought down Richard Nixon's presidency.

item.description: US Vice President JD Vance says it is "crazy" that the Watergate scandal brought down Richard Nixon's presidency, arguing that the political spying affair would dominate headlines for no more than a few hours.
item.content:encoded: US Vice President JD Vance says it is "crazy" that the Watergate scandal brought down Richard Nixon's presidency, arguing that the political spying affair would dominate headlines for no more than a few hours.

item.title: Israel To Remain In South Lebanon Until Hezbollah Disarms: Netanyahu

media:content.media:description: Netanyahu added that Israel's military would also allow the Lebanese army to control territory.

item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Friday that Israel would remain in south Lebanon until Hezbollah disarms, shortly after the announcement in Washington of a framework agreement with the United States and Lebanon.
item.content:encoded: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Friday that Israel would remain in south Lebanon until Hezbollah disarms, shortly after the announcement in Washington of a framework agreement with the United States and Lebanon.

item.title: Netherlands Euthanises Sick Child Under 12 For First Time In History

media:content.media:description: Dutch Health Minister Sophie Hermans confirmed that the child died last year

item.description: The Netherlands introduced a legal change in 2024 allowing euthanasia for children aged between 1 and 12 who are facing severe and unbearable suffering from a terminal illness.
item.content:encoded: The Netherlands introduced a legal change in 2024 allowing euthanasia for children aged between 1 and 12 who are facing severe and unbearable suffering from a terminal illness.

item.title: In Breakthrough, Lebanon, Israel And US Announce Trilateral Agreement

media:content.media:description: The deal is aimed at lasting peace in the Middle East.

item.description: The agreement "begins to put in place a framework for lasting peace and security," US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said.
item.content:encoded: The agreement "begins to put in place a framework for lasting peace and security," US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said.

item.title: 42 Ships Cross Strait Of Hormuz Using Oman Passage Despite Iran Threats

media:content.media:description: Since June 15, traffic has been steadily increasing through the strait.

item.description: Traffic levels in the Strait of Hormuz fell back from Wednesday's high, but vessels continued to use a non-Iranian-approved passage despite a ship being struck by a projectile, tracking platforms showed.
item.content:encoded: Traffic levels in the Strait of Hormuz fell back from Wednesday's high, but vessels continued to use a non-Iranian-approved passage despite a ship being struck by a projectile, tracking platforms showed.

item.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Series in US-Only Preview At Government's Request

media:content.media:description: OpenAI said in a blog post that the limited release was a temporary step.

item.description: By securing early access to frontier models, US officials are aiming to identify threats ranging from cyberattacks to military misuse before the tools are widely deployed.
item.content:encoded: By securing early access to frontier models, US officials are aiming to identify threats ranging from cyberattacks to military misuse before the tools are widely deployed.

item.title: Israel Declassifies Documents On Daring 1976 Entebbe Hostage Rescue

media:content.media:description: Israel released the files ahead of the raid's 50th anniversary on July 3.

item.description: The operation saw dozens of Israeli commandos storm the Entebbe Airport, where Palestinian and German militants - backed by Ugandan forces - had taken 106 passengers from a hijacked flight from Tel Aviv to Paris.
item.content:encoded: The operation saw dozens of Israeli commandos storm the Entebbe Airport, where Palestinian and German militants - backed by Ugandan forces - had taken 106 passengers from a hijacked flight from Tel Aviv to Paris.

item.title: UAE Discusses Hormuz Security With Iran In Rare Call Amid Ceasefire

media:content.media:description: It was the first announced conversation between the two countries since US and Israeli strikes on Iran.

item.description: The exchange, reported by state news agency WAM, marks a rare public contact between Abu Dhabi and Tehran following tensions linked to the US-Israeli war on Iran.
item.content:encoded: The exchange, reported by state news agency WAM, marks a rare public contact between Abu Dhabi and Tehran following tensions linked to the US-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Death Count In Venezuela Quake Climbs To 920, Over 3,300 injured

media:content.media:description: The devastating 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes struck late Wednesday.

item.description: Rodriguez declared a state of emergency in an address to the nation late Wednesday. She said the government was creating a $200 million reconstruction fund for damaged hospitals and homes.
item.content:encoded: Rodriguez declared a state of emergency in an address to the nation late Wednesday. She said the government was creating a $200 million reconstruction fund for damaged hospitals and homes.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: Deaths From Venezuela Earthquakes Climbs To At Least 920 Dead

media:content.media:description: 7.5 Magnitude Earthquake in Venezuela Updates: The first earthquake had a magnitude of 7.2

item.description: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.
item.content:encoded: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.

item.title: German City Sizzles At 41.3 Degrees, Country's Highest-Ever Temperature

media:content.media:description: The DWD cautioned that the data could be revised down.

item.description: The extreme forecast has already prompted the cancellation of numerous events over the weekend, including concerts, festivals and sporting competitions.
item.content:encoded: The extreme forecast has already prompted the cancellation of numerous events over the weekend, including concerts, festivals and sporting competitions.

item.title: Rs 7,744 Crore A Year: Indian-Origin CEO Highest-Paid Executive After Musk

media:content.media:description: Mitra's pay package was surpassed only by Musk, whose compensation was $158 billion

item.description: Shankh Mitra's rise began in Kolkata, where he studied engineering at Jadavpur University, completing a Bachelor of Engineering degree in Instrumentation and Electronics Engineering.
item.content:encoded: Shankh Mitra's rise began in Kolkata, where he studied engineering at Jadavpur University, completing a Bachelor of Engineering degree in Instrumentation and Electronics Engineering.

item.title: UK Breaks Record Hottest June Day Record For 3rd Time This Year

media:content.media:description: The Met Office has issued its highest level red "extreme heat" warning.

item.description: The 36.9 degrees Celsius record temperature was chalked in Wattisham, Suffolk, in southeast England, the meteorologists said, breaking the previous high of 36.7 degrees Celsius set on Thursday.
item.content:encoded: The 36.9 degrees Celsius record temperature was chalked in Wattisham, Suffolk, in southeast England, the meteorologists said, breaking the previous high of 36.7 degrees Celsius set on Thursday.

item.title: Overflowing Hospitals, Cancelled Festivals: Europe Buckles Under Heatwave

media:content.media:description: France saw a fourfold increase in heat-related hospital visits.

item.description: Health authorities in Britain and France warned hospitals were struggling with the heat and a surge in emergency calls.
item.content:encoded: Health authorities in Britain and France warned hospitals were struggling with the heat and a surge in emergency calls.

item.title: Kremlin To Look Into "Strange" Video Appeal To Putin Alleging Army Torture

media:content.media:description: The video garnered over 12 million views in 24 hours. (File)

item.description: The viral video appeals to President Vladimir Putin by a military veteran accusing Russian commanders in Ukraine of torturing and murdering soldiers for not fulfilling "suicidal orders".
item.content:encoded: The viral video appeals to President Vladimir Putin by a military veteran accusing Russian commanders in Ukraine of torturing and murdering soldiers for not fulfilling "suicidal orders".

item.title: Trump Threatens 100% Tariff On European Countries Over Digital Services Tax

media:content.media:description: The threat comes ahead of Trump's July 4 deadline for the European Union and US to approve a tariff deal.

item.description: Trump took aim at European countries that he said are discussing "imminent" implementation of taxes on American companies.
item.content:encoded: Trump took aim at European countries that he said are discussing "imminent" implementation of taxes on American companies.
